name: Alto Tajo and Arbeteta Castle Loop
name_original: Aguas del Río Tajo: Castillo de Arbeteta - desde Villanueva de Alcorón — circular por el Paraje Natural Alto Tajo
description: This difficult 72.4-mile road cycling route through the Alto Tajo Natural Area offers dramatic landscapes and views of Arbeteta Castle.
region_intro: Road cycling around Villanueva De Alcorón provides routes through the Alto Tajo Natural Park, an area defined by deeply carved Tajo River canyons and extensive pine forests. The terrain is karstic, with limestone moorlands and a high altitude, contributing to challenging ascents and descents. Cyclists can expect varied landscapes, including views of the Tajo River and its surrounding geological features. Best road cycling routes around Villanueva De Alcorón The most popular road cycling route is Alto Tajo and Arbeteta Castle Loop, a 72.4 miles (116.6 km) difficult trail that takes about 5 hours 49 minutes to complete. This route offers expansive views of the Alto Tajo region and passes by historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Molina de Aragón Castle – Molina de Aragón loop from Peñalén, a difficult 68.1 miles (109.6 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and scenic sections through the natural park. Local road cyclists also love the Peñalén – Hoz de Beteta loop from Armallones, a 77.8 miles (125.3 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 6 hours 1 minute. This challenging route explores canyons and forested areas. Road cycling around Villanueva De Alcorón is defined by high-altitude terrain, deep river canyons, and extensive pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, though many routes feature significant elevation gains. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 50 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villanueva De Alcorón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: There are currently 4 highly-rated road cycling routes available around Villanueva De Alcorón, offering diverse experiences through the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: The terrain around Villanueva De Alcorón is characterized by the deeply carved canyons of the Tajo River, extensive pine forests, and limestone moorlands. Expect significant elevation changes, challenging climbs, and exhilarating descents, all set against a backdrop of impressive natural scenery. The urban center itself is perched at approximately 1,200 meters, contributing to the high-altitude cycling experience.

Q: Are there routes suitable for different fitness levels?
A: While many routes in the area feature significant elevation gains and are rated as difficult, there is at least one moderate route available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén – Narrow pass near the Tajo River loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt; offers a less strenuous option while still showcasing the region's beauty.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy spectacular views of the Tajo River and its canyons. Notable natural features include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5373777" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail along the Tajo River&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3548956" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tormo de la Muesca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, and the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7249954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Tormellera Gorge&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features karstic terrain with sinkholes and caves, such as the Sima de Alcorón, and the unique artificial lagoon, Pozo del Soto.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural points of interest accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, some routes pass by historical landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018257941/alto-tajo-and-arbeteta-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Tajo and Arbeteta Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views and passes by historical sites. The area also retains old lime kilns (caleras), which are remnants of traditional industry.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: Villanueva De Alcorón, as a small village, typically offers street parking. While specific dedicated cyclist parking facilities may be limited, you can usually find places to park within the village before starting your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling around Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: Given its high altitude, the best time for road cycling around Villanueva De Alcorón is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be warm, but the altitude often provides some relief. Winters can be cold and potentially snowy, making cycling more challenging.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Molina de Aragón Castle – Molina de Aragón loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143088" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén – Hoz de Beteta loop from Armallones&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are the roads mostly paved for road bikes?
A: Yes, the routes highlighted for road cycling in this guide primarily feature paved surfaces, making them suitable for road bikes. While the Alto Tajo Natural Park has various paths, these specific routes are selected for their road bike compatibility.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the routes or in Villanueva De Alcorón?
A: Villanueva De Alcorón itself, being a charming mountain village, offers local establishments where you can find refreshments. Along the longer routes, options may be sparse, so it's advisable to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when venturing into more remote sections of the Alto Tajo Natural Park.

Q: What accommodation options are available for cyclists in the area?
A: Villanueva De Alcorón and nearby villages within the Alto Tajo Natural Park offer various rural accommodations, including guesthouses and small hotels, catering to visitors exploring the region. It's recommended to book in advance, especially during peak seasons.

Q: What are the typical distances and elevation gains for road cycling routes here?
A: Road cycling routes around Villanueva De Alcorón vary in length, with many being long-distance tours. For example, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1018257941/alto-tajo-and-arbeteta-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto Tajo and Arbeteta Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; cover over 116 km with more than 1800 meters of elevation gain, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39559611" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñalén – Narrow pass near the Tajo River loop from Peñalén&lt;/a&gt; is around 62 km with over 600 meters of ascent.
tips: Narrow pass that is near the Casas del Campillo, where the road runs through the same stone. The walls form atypical horizontal walls on the roads in the area. Nearby, you can see some of the meanders of the Tagus River.
In this place, the Tagus, barely born, forms a small waterfall. A place of great beauty and accessible by car if we want to start the route here

The Valtablado del Río bridge crosses the waters of the Tagus in an area full of poplars. The great width and shallowness of the river, added to the shade provided by the trees, creates a privileged bathing area on hot days.
Circulating near the town of Arbeteta, at one of its ends and next to the Arroyo de la Rambla, is the Castle elevated on top of a huge rock. Spectacular when you go under it.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty and terrain like for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is officially rated as difficult, covering approximately 116.6 km with over 1,800 meters of elevation gain. While listed for racebikes, the terrain in the Alto Tajo Natural Area is known for significant climbs and descents, and may include unpaved or rough sections. It is likely more suitable for gravel or mountain bikes due to these challenging conditions.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: As you cycle, you'll encounter several notable features. Keep an eye out for the Narrow pass near the Tajo River, the historic San Pedro Bridge, and the picturesque Valtablado del Río Bridge. You'll also get stunning Views of Arbeteta Castle, offering a glimpse into the region's history.

faqs.question: Where can I park my vehicle when starting this circular route from Villanueva de Alcorón?
faqs.answer: Villanueva de Alcorón serves as the starting point for this circular route. While specific parking facilities for cyclists aren't detailed, you should be able to find suitable parking within or on the outskirts of the village.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Alto Tajo Natural Area?
faqs.answer: The route passes through the Alto Tajo Natural Area, a protected natural park. Generally, there are no entrance fees for the park itself. However, it's always advisable to check the official website for the Parque Natural del Alto Tajo for any specific regulations or permits that might apply to cycling, especially for large groups or certain activities.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Alto Tajo Natural Area offers dramatic landscapes that are beautiful year-round. However, for cycling, spring and autumn typically provide the most pleasant weather, with milder temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder conditions and potential for snow at higher elevations, making cycling more challenging.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for families or beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its difficult rating, significant distance (over 116 km), and substantial elevation gain (over 1,800 m), this particular route is not recommended for beginners or families with young children. It requires very good fitness and experience with challenging terrain.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: While the Alto Tajo Natural Area is generally welcoming to visitors, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's best to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety, especially given the challenging nature of the terrain and potential for traffic on some sections.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the Tajo River?
faqs.answer: The route offers immersion in the wild and dramatic landscapes of the Alto Tajo Natural Area. You'll experience deep gorges carved by the 'wild river' Tajo, extensive black pine woods, lush riparian forests, and impressive cliffs. Expect panoramic views from elevated points and a rich natural environment.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Natural del Tajo (11%) and MontañasVacías (8%). You'll also briefly encounter parts of the Rambla del Avellano (Arbeteta), Georuta 1 «Ocentejo-Hundido de Armallones», and Ruta Hundido de Armallones a Salinas y Pico Alar.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or accommodation in Villanueva de Alcorón?
faqs.answer: Villanueva de Alcorón is a small village, and while it may offer basic amenities, it's advisable to check in advance for specific cafes, restaurants, or accommodation options. It's a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks for the duration of this long and challenging ride.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's difficulty and length, essential gear includes a well-maintained bike (preferably gravel or mountain bike), a helmet, appropriate cycling attire, plenty of water and nutrition, a repair kit, a first-aid kit, and a fully charged phone. Navigation tools are also crucial, as some sections may be remote.

waypoints.name: Narrow pass near the Tajo River
